commit.message:
Upgrade violin synth to additive harmonics and realistic bow physics

Synth: 12-partial additive synthesis with measured violin harmonic
profile, bow-noise layer (pink-noise rosin friction), dual body
resonance formants (A0 air + B1+ wood modes), bridge hill brilliance
peak, staggered harmonic attack, and amplitude vibrato.

Bow: curved pernambuco stick with frog/ferrule/winding/tip, sinusoidal
easing for stroke acceleration, pressure-based height, tilt at
direction changes, sympathetic string resonance glow, and smooth
lift-off animation.

nested ExceptionGroups. Catch and suppress these for clean shutdown.

commit.message:
fix(ci): resolve missing Python modules and AWS region configuration

Fixes failing CI/CD tests by:
1. Adding proper PYTHONPATH to include backend and lambda directories
   - export PYTHONPATH="${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/backend:${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/backend/lambda:$PYTHONPATH"

2. Setting AWS_REGION environment variables
   - AWS_REGION: us-east-1
   - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1

3. Correcting test path from tests/ to lambda/tests/

This resolves:
- ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'lambda_'
- ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'compliance'
- botocore.exceptions.NoRegionError: You must specify a region

Affects: Backend Tests (Python) job

commit.message:
Fix photo downloads: proper headers, logging, URL validation, og:image fallback

Root cause: Claude's web search returns 27 mixed content blocks (tool_use +
tool_result + text) — our simple [→] JSON extraction was grabbing search snippet
arrays instead of the final photo list, resulting in count=0 before any download.

Fixes:
- Two-step approach: Step 1 does web search research (free-form text), Step 2 is a
  separate no-tools call that extracts URLs into clean JSON — eliminates the
  mixed-block parsing problem
- downloadAndStoreImage: add proper Chrome User-Agent + Accept headers, redirect:follow
- Per-URL logging: log each URL attempted, HTTP status, content-type, and error
- URL validation: skip data:/blob: URLs, require http/https prefix
- Non-image content-type: log warning but still attempt storage (some CDNs use
  generic content types for valid images)
- og:image fallback: if all downloads fail, fetch school homepage, parse og:image
  meta tag, download that single image as a last resort
- Error message now includes candidate count for better debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Docker, GitHub Actions CI, pinned requirements, and test suite

Dockerfile
- Multi-stage build: builder (gcc + pip install) → runner (slim runtime)
- libgomp1 installed in runner for XGBoost OpenMP support on Linux
- Non-root appuser (uid 1001) for security
- HEALTHCHECK polls GET /health every 30 s (3 retries, 15 s start period)
- Exposes port 8000; uvicorn entrypoint with --workers 1

.dockerignore
- Excludes data/, mlruns/, .git/, __pycache__, .env, *.md, .github/

.github/workflows/ci.yml
- Triggers on push/PR to main; concurrency group cancels stale runs
- Job 1 lint: flake8 --max-line-length=88 --extend-ignore=E203,W503
- Job 2 test (needs: lint): generates dataset → pytest --cov=src --cov=api
  --cov-fail-under=70 → uploads coverage.xml artifact → posts PR comment
  via MishaKav/pytest-coverage-comment
- Job 3 build (needs: test): Docker Buildx with layer cache → smoke-tests
  the image by polling /health until the server is ready

requirements.txt
- Pinned to exact compatible versions; comments group by concern
- mlflow pinned to 2.13.2 (3.x has Python 3.11 wheel issues)

tests/ (57 tests, 0 warnings, run locally against Python 3.9)
- test_preprocess.py: load_data, clean_data, engineer_features, split_and_scale
- test_predict.py: load_model, build_feature_row, risk_level, predict
- test_api.py: /health, /predict (happy + all 422/503 error paths), /metrics

api/main.py
- Replaced deprecated @app.on_event("startup") with lifespan context manager

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enforce intent logging across all agents

Three-layer intent capture ensures no agent can skip logging:

1. MCP tool description marks aura_log_intent as MANDATORY — agents
   that read tool descriptions will always call it
2. MCP responses include intent reminder if agent hasn't logged yet
   (via .aura/.intent_logged session marker)
3. Pre-commit fallback chain: .gemini.intent → commit message →
   .aura/intent_log.jsonl — so even without MCP, intent is captured

Also: aura_log_intent now persists to both .gemini.intent (for hook)
and .aura/intent_log.jsonl (for watcher daemon snapshot linking).

commit.message:
feat: replace heuristic LOC estimates with precise cloc counts for all benchmark repos

The old repo_approx_loc was computed as repo_size_bytes/30, which inflated
values by 5-50x (e.g. linux: 208M heuristic vs 29.8M actual). Now uses
cloc --json on shallow clones of all 71 repos.

- Add scripts/collect_repo_cloc.py: shallow-clone + cloc pipeline (resumable)
- Add scripts/update_loc_from_cloc.py: propagate cloc values to all configs
- Update backfill_size_metadata.py to prefer cloc data over bytes/30 heuristic
- Update 470/477 tasks across 9 config files + official_results.json
- 71 repos counted: 9.9K (grpcurl) to 43.4M (chromium) code lines

beat a lower-tier top row (e.g. Suited) regardless of raw points.
Previously, top pts > bottom pts was flagged as Sabsuch even when the
bottom had a stronger hand type, blocking valid high-multiplier splits.

  to agents but recoverable

  now inspects sqlite_master schema SQL directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize deep search with pre-built session index (30x faster)

Build a HashMap<PathBuf, Session> at startup mapping JSONL file paths
to their pre-loaded Session objects. Deep search now does:
1. Scan files for regex matches (fast, parallel with rayon)
2. Look up matching files in the index (O(1) HashMap lookup)
3. Fall back to file parsing only for undiscovered sessions

Results for 2170 sessions / 753 MB:
- "function" (903 matches): 16.5s -> 0.55s (30x faster)
- Uncommon terms: ~0.4s (no regression)
- No matches: ~0.3s (no regression)

The session index is shared via Arc for thread-safe access from
the background search thread.

Assisted-By: 🤖 Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add data export (CSV/XLSX), conversation management, validation boost, and audit fixes

Features:
- export_data agent tool: structured data export as CSV or Excel with styled headers
- Download endpoint (GET /api/v1/chat/downloads/{id}) with TTL-based file registry
- Conversation export as markdown (GET) and reset with feedback cleanup (DELETE)
- Validated retrieval feedback loop: query-aware boost (+0.15/+0.10/+0.05)
- Confidence scoring integrates validation status (has_validated → HIGH)
- UI: PDF/Markdown export buttons, download button rendering, validation badge updates

Security & audit fixes:
- Constant-time password comparison (secrets.compare_digest)
- XSS-safe download button rendering (textContent instead of innerHTML)
- Export row/column limits (10K rows, 200 columns) to prevent DoS
- Input validation on export_data tool handler
- Numeric coercion preserves leading zeros and rejects scientific notation
- Specific Anthropic API error surfacing (auth, rate limit, connection)

Tests:
- 21 new tests for exports module (coercion, CSV/XLSX gen, registry, limits)
- Fixed stale CORS origins test (now checks membership, not exact list)
- Fixed stale validator test (dynamic package count, water-infra exceptions)
- All 126 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify audio player, deepen the 100-bit wall with bandwidth data

Audio:
- One audio per page: section pages filter to matching audio only
- AudioPlayer rewritten as minimal: play/pause, progress bar, time
- Removed section dropdown, prev/next section, multi-section state
- ~200 lines removed from AudioPlayer

Content (Part VI, The 100-Bit Wall):
- Conscious output bandwidth: 10-40 bits/sec depending on modality
  (speech ~12, typing ~8-12, mouse ~3-5, expert music ~30-40)
- Retina streams 10M bits/sec; consciousness compresses to hundreds
  of effective dimensions — consciousness IS the compression
- Compression quality varies by evolutionary depth: spatial (200M yrs,
  exceptional) through screen interaction (40 yrs, poor)
- New sidebar: "Compression Quality by Evolutionary Depth" with the
  full domain × evolutionary depth × quality table
- Implication: high-throughput interfaces should target motor system's
  500M years of optimization, not screen's 40 years

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: force code_exec for all analytical categories (override UCB1 bandit)

The UCB1 Router starts in explore mode and may select llm_direct for
analytical categories. Python code execution is always more accurate
than direct LLM for numeric aggregation, date computation, etc.

Hard override: if category in _CRM_ANALYTICAL_CATEGORIES → strategy = code_exec
regardless of what the Router suggests. The Router's reward signal will
still be recorded, but the strategy is deterministic for analytics.

commit.message:
fix(add): accept PMTiles as cloud-native primary asset (#201)

* fix(add): accept PMTiles as cloud-native primary asset (#198)

PMTiles (.pmtiles) was listed in CLOUD_NATIVE_EXTENSIONS in formats.py
but two other modules classified it differently, causing 'portolan add'
to fail with "Unsupported format: .pmtiles":

- scan_classify.py: .pmtiles was in VIZ_EXTENSIONS → VISUALIZATION
  category, so scan skipped it as "visualization-only, not primary data"
- scan.py: .pmtiles was in OVERVIEW_EXTENSIONS → explicitly skipped as
  a derivative/overview format, not added to RECOGNIZED_VECTOR_EXTENSIONS

Fix: move .pmtiles from VIZ_EXTENSIONS/OVERVIEW_EXTENSIONS into
GEO_ASSET_EXTENSIONS and RECOGNIZED_VECTOR_EXTENSIONS, matching the
treatment of FlatGeobuf (.fgb) — both are extension-based cloud-native
formats that pass through without conversion.

Tests updated to reflect the corrected behavior:
- test_pmtiles_classified_as_visualization → test_pmtiles_classified_as_geo_asset
- test_pmtiles_files_are_skipped → test_pmtiles_files_are_accepted_as_primary_assets
- test_pmtiles_does_not_count_as_primary → test_pmtiles_counts_as_primary_alongside_geoparquet

New regression tests added:
- test_pmtiles_in_geo_asset_extensions
- test_pmtiles_not_in_viz_extensions
- test_pmtiles_not_in_overview_extensions
- test_pmtiles_scanned_as_ready_file
- test_mbtiles_still_classified_as_visualization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(formats): add PMTiles to VECTOR_EXTENSIONS for detect_format()

The original fix missed that add_dataset() calls detect_format() which
returned UNKNOWN for PMTiles because it wasn't in VECTOR_EXTENSIONS.
This caused the "Unsupported format: .pmtiles" error to persist.

PMTiles is now in VECTOR_EXTENSIONS (like FlatGeobuf), so:
1. detect_format() returns VECTOR
2. add_dataset() calls convert_vector()
3. convert_vector() checks cloud-native status → CLOUD_NATIVE
4. Conversion is skipped, file is tracked successfully

Also adds:
- Unit tests for detect_format() with PMTiles and FlatGeobuf
- Unit tests for VECTOR_EXTENSIONS membership
- Hypothesis property-based tests for PMTiles consistency
- Hypothesis tests for cloud-native vector format parity

Fixes #198 (adversarial review finding)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

demo: fix OpenAI CORS via Vite dev-server proxy

OpenAI does not send CORS headers, so direct browser → api.openai.com
requests fail with 'Failed to fetch'.

Fix:
- Add Vite server.proxy: /llm-proxy/openai → https://api.openai.com
  Routes the call server-to-server (Vite ↔ OpenAI), bypassing CORS.
- In llm.ts, use import.meta.env.DEV to switch base URL:
    DEV:  /llm-proxy/openai  (Vite proxy)
    PROD: https://api.openai.com  (direct; needs a backend proxy in prod)
- Anthropic is unchanged (uses anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access).
- Ollama is unchanged (local, no CORS issues).
- Add contextual error hints in App.tsx error state:
    "Failed to fetch" → remind user to run npm run dev / switch provider
    401/403 / Invalid  → suggest checking the API key
